title: linanthus dichotomus subsp. dichotomus
intro: Linanthus dichotomus subsp. dichotomus, also known as twinflower, is a flowering plant species native to the southwestern United States and northern Mexico. It is a perennial herb that has been traditionally used in herbal medicine for its antimicrobial and antiseptic properties. The plant's leaves and flowers are used to treat various health issues, including wounds, skin infections, and respiratory problems, due to its anti-inflammatory and expectorant properties. Twinflower can be prepared as a tea, tincture, or salve, and its health benefits include wound healing, reduced inflammation, and relief from respiratory congestion.
